instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Should you Buy a Critical Illness or a Disease-Specific Plan
Meta Description: Read on to learn the significance and distinction between Critical Illness cover and Disease-specific Plans so you can make an informed decision.
Keywords: critical illness cover, critical illness plan, critical illness insurance_____________________________________________________________________________________
With so many alternatives to pick from in India, finding the perfect insurance policy might be difficult. The sort of insurance policy you select is critical in obtaining the appropriate coverage for your specific needs. With people's evolving needs, several insurance firms now provide critical illness insurance and disease-specific insurance coverage. These plans assist you in obtaining financial protection in the event of a medical emergency.
Which policy should you choose if you're unsure? Here are a few key points to remember regarding these policies.
Disease-Specific Insurance Policy
A condition-specific insurance plan, as the name implies, covers the whole cost of treatment for a certain ailment at all stages, from diagnosis to advanced therapy. One of the most important aspects of this insurance plan is that the insurance companies waive future premiums upon disease diagnosis, according to the terms and conditions of the policy.
Furthermore, unlike the critical illness plan, the disease-specific coverage has a lower premium because it only covers one ailment. This makes it an excellent choice of insurance cover for those searching for a low-cost policy that protects them against a specific disease to which they are predisposed. This form of insurance may also be appropriate if you have a family history of a certain disease, such as cancer.
What is the waiting period for a disease-specific plan?
One of the primary reasons why many people prefer to get disease-specific insurance policies is that there is no waiting period. This means that you can file a claim as soon as you are diagnosed with the sickness. After validating the claim, the insurer will pay the amounts insured.
What is the ideal sum assured for disease-specific insurance?
If you want to receive disease-specific insurance, you must first decide the disease you want to cover. Experts often advise purchasing an insurance plan with a sum assured of around Rs 10 lakhs. However, if you want more financial security, you can get a plan with a bigger sum assured. Similarly to investing, educating oneself about the correct insurance is critical to making the right decision.
What is covered under the disease-specific plan?
A disease-specific insurance plan can be purchased for a variety of disorders. Recently, insurance firms have begun to offer disease-specific insurance plans for pandemic and dengue. If you are diagnosed with the sickness, the insurance company will pay the sum assured in a lump payment to cover your medical costs.
Critical Illness Insurance Policy
A critical illness insurance policy is meant to cover specific potentially life-threatening disorders such as cancer, heart attack, serious burns, organ transplant, stroke, and so on. Following the diagnosis of the conditions covered by the plan, the insurance company pays a lump sum amount equal to the sum insured. You can utilise the funds to cover medical costs, post-hospitalization fees, and even debt repayment. The amount can be used however the insurer sees fit.
What is the waiting period for a critical illness plan?
Before filing a claim under a critical illness plan, the insured must survive for at least 30 days following the diagnosis of the critical disease. However, some insurers offer insurance with a surviving period of 0 to 28 days. In terms of waiting time, most critical illness plans have a 90-day waiting period. This means that claims made within 90 days of the policy's issuance are not covered by the policy.
What is the ideal sum assured for critical illness insurance?
The cost of critical sickness care can occasionally reach several lakhs. To provide adequate coverage, you must examine crucial criteria such as age, family history, and so on, and determine the sum assured accordingly. An insurance with a sum assured of Rs 15-20 lakhs is regarded as ideal in general.
What is covered under the critical illness plan?
Most insurance companies cover between 8-20 ailments under the critical illness plan. Furthermore, while the plan provides more comprehensive coverage than disease-specific insurance, the premium is greater.
Sources:
https://www.livemint.com/insurance/news/opinion-should-you-buy-a-critical-illness-or-a-disease-specific-plan-1552833320069.html
